findings.evidence: The file defines `I` at line 80, `JNum` at line 126, and `J` at line 134; `rg -n "I_(union|insert|disj|sdiff)|J(Num)?_(union|insert|disj|sdiff|add|sub)" TauCeti` returns no hits, while the roadmap target states `M_O(x) = J(x−O, x−O)` and grading-change formulas.
findings.fix: Add characteristic splitting lemmas such as `I_union_left/right` under disjointness, singleton/insert variants if that is the local normal form, and induced `JNum`/`J` additivity lemmas for the same operations.
findings.issue: `GridPoint.I`/`JNum`/`J` expose point-set counts but provide no union/insert additivity API, so the later `J(x-O,x-O)` formal-difference grading work cannot use the count bilinearly without unfolding `I`.

summary: The basic defining and specialization lemmas are present, but the public point-set API is still missing the splitting/additivity lemmas needed by the stated grading target. Downstream formal-difference and rectangle-change proofs would have to unfold the filtered product count directly.
